title: 2026 01 11 Rankett Meetup Prep Gdocs
html:
TL;DR

  • A 4-day revision of the Jan 7 prep doc for the same GEO & AI Visibility Meetup, January 2026 — same skeleton, sharpened in places.
  • Same 10-section "Understanding Rankett" preamble + same 50-question defense bank (the Q&A bank is the load-bearing artifact, not the prose).
  • Confirms the ghost white label commitment by repeating it verbatim — "Zero Rankett branding anywhere. Not in the UI, not in the page source, not in meta tags, not in error messages."
  • Restates the 60 day activation rule and 5+ client minimum, framing them as quality filters that protect the pooled data community from dilution.
  • Tech stack reiterated (Next.js, Supabase, OpenAI / Claude / Gemini APIs) — agency partners "never touch any of this; it's all abstracted behind the platform."

What's actually new vs. Jan 7

Reading them side by side, the revisions are mostly polish — Yahir was rehearsing the talk track. Notable deltas:

  • The "Old Model → New Model" comparison is the same set of bullets but the new wording reads less defensive.
  • The 50 Q&A keeps the same structure but Q14 (reporting cadence) explicitly anchors "weekly reports for the first 4 weeks to show fast proof" — the speed-to-proof posture from the apple notes GTM bible survives.
  • "Prompts are the new keywords" moves from a tossed-off phrase to a positioning anchor.

Why two doc versions four days apart

The pattern matches the broader solo founder loop — same content rebuilt on different surfaces (apple-notes → Google Doc → revised Google Doc). The Google-Docs revisions are evidence of rehearsal, not invention. By Jan 13 the same playbook is being shoved into the rankett.com junk drawer.

Connections
